description:
GOAL: Qualitative validation that inlining actually helps and didn't break
anything in the wider system. Final ticket to close the .15 arc.

Parent: fz-ul4.11.15. Depends on: fz-ul4.11.15.5.

## Deliverables

### 1. Hot-loop allocation assertion

  Add a thread-local alloc counter in src/ir_codegen.rs runtime hooks:

    thread_local! {
        pub static FRAME_ALLOC_COUNT: Cell<u64> = Cell::new(0);
    }
    // increment in fz_alloc_frame (the existing FFI hook)

    pub fn frame_alloc_count_reset() -> u64;
    pub fn frame_alloc_count_take() -> u64;

  New test in ir_inline.rs (or tests/ir_inline_hot_loop.rs):

    fn step(x), do: x + 1
    fn loop(0, acc), do: acc
    fn loop(n, acc), do: loop(n - 1, step(acc))

    main:
      loop(1000, 0)

  Pre-inline: loop calls step via Call+Cont each iteration -> N cont-frame
  allocations.
  Post-inline: step is inlined into loop -> per-iter alloc drops dramatically.

  Assert: post-inline frame_alloc_count is ≤ 50% of a pre-inline baseline
  (or a small absolute number, e.g. < 10 — depending on how loop itself is
  compiled).

  NOTE: count(100000, 0) self-recursion already triggers .11.8 tail-call
  frame REUSE (same-schema in-place overwrite — see codegen.rs:2197). So
  pure self-recursive counts already alloc zero per iter. The hot-loop test
  above uses a separate `step` fn to stress the inliner's contribution.

### 2. SAMPLE parity

  Run main.rs's SAMPLE constant through ir_codegen (existing test harness
  for `fz run` smoke). Capture stdout. Snapshot-match against the
  pre-inlining baseline. Any divergence is a correctness regression.

### 3. ir_interp parity property test

  Build 5-10 hand-crafted IR Modules covering:
    - leaf inlinable callee at TailCall site
    - leaf inlinable callee at Call+Cont site
    - closure with known MakeClosure
    - non-leaf callee (not inlined)
    - over-budget callee (not inlined)
    - chain a → b → c
    - main with no inlinable sites at all (parity trivially)

  For each: run ir_interp on a fresh clone. Run inline_module + ir_interp.
  Assert equal output Values.

### 4. GC root invariant

  Allocate a heap value (Vec) inside an inlined hot loop, force a GC, assert
  contents readable. NOTE: if ir_codegen currently has no test hook for
  forcing GC, this becomes a written-but-skipped test with a note pointing
  to fz-ul4.11 (the tracing GC parent). If a GC trigger exists, exercise it.

### 5. Closeout

  - All existing tests pass.
  - bw close fz-ul4.11.15.6 → then bw close fz-ul4.11.15.

## Acceptance

  - Hot-loop alloc count drops ≥50%.
  - SAMPLE output unchanged.
  - All parity tests pass.
  - Test count grows; no regressions.
  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.15.6.

## References

  - src/main.rs:31 (SAMPLE constant).
  - src/ir_codegen.rs runtime FFI hooks (fz_alloc_frame).
  - fz-ul4.11 (Tracing GC parent) for GC trigger context.
title: Hot-loop allocation assert + SAMPLE parity + closeout
